entry.title: Simplifying Data Navigation: Leveraging the SAP BW Report-Report Interface in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Since many years SAP is offering the SAP BW Report-Report Interface (RRI) functionality in its products like Lumira or Analysis for Office.  

With the upcoming 2024 QRC3 release (roadmap), we are happy to announce that the RRI will be supported in SAP Analytics Cloud.  

What is the Report-Report Interface

At its core, RRI enables users to link different reports together and provides seamless navigation between reports. For instance, starting with a high-level summary report, you can drill down into more detailed information without losing the data context.

Getting Started with RRI: Prerequisites and Setup

Before using the RRI feature in SAP Analytics Cloud, you need to apply SAP Note 3439944 on your SAP BW system. You can do this by using SNOTE or by patching your system to the minimum support package mentioned in the note.

Once your system is ready, activate the RSADMIN parameter "INA_RRI_ENABLE" and set it to "X". To create, change, or delete RSADMIN parameters, use the report "SAP_RSADMIN_MAINTAIN" (SE38). Keep in mind that this parameter might change in future versions of the RRI feature and support.

Now that the SAP BW system is configured, you can start defining jump targets. Use transaction RSBBS to specify the sender report, define the target receiver report, and outline the assignment details.

Tcode RSBBS

That’s it, you have enabled the RRI and can use it in SAP Analytics Cloud.

In the first phase of the RRI support in SAP Analytics Cloud, the following static scenarios will be included:

  • BW Queries (will be opened in Data Analyzer)
  • Web Addresses (https only)
  • ABAP Transactions
  • ABAP Reports

Jump targets are supported on tables of the Optimized Story Experience or Data Analyzer when using a SAP BW Live connection.

Integrate RRI targets in SAP Analytics Cloud

Good news! You don't need to develop or maintain anything in SAP Analytics Cloud to use the RRI. The magic happens entirely in the SAP BW backend during the sender/receiver assignment process.

When using RRI, jump targets are offered via the context menu. Right-click on a cell in your report; if jump targets are available, they'll be listed under the "Jump To" entry.

RRI in SAP Analytics Cloud

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Great News! With the latest releases of 2024.14 for the SAP Analytics Cloud fast track customers (BI weekly release model) and for Quarterly release cycle with 2024 Q3 QRC, SAP Analytics Cloud, Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint is now Generally available.

https://appsource.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/WA200007270?tab=Overview 

 

 

1. What is SAP Analytics Cloud, Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint?

Microsoft Office tools like PowerPoint and Excel are widely used tools. Integration and Embedding of SAC chart widgets into your business application will help customers to make confident decisions without switching the context.

Launching the New SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint, You can now embed the SAP Analytics Cloud story widgets into PowerPoint as an Image which can be dynamically updated with the latest Data.

Business Value:

  1. Streamline Presentation Updates: Instead of manually copying and pasting Widgets from SAP Analytics Cloud for every presentation, create the PowerPoint once and refresh only when needed. This approach is perfect for recurrent presentations.
  2. Efficient Data Management: Save different versions of your presentations to maintain a data history.

With SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint you can:

  • Seamlessly connect to an SAP Analytics Cloud tenant
  • Quickly find SAP Analytics Cloud stories and related widgets
  • Select a widget and filter its data, if necessary
  • Add the widget to the slides.
  • Refresh the widget's data any time on demand.

Note: The initial Version of SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint supports only Microsoft Office 365 Web Version and Support for the desktop app is planned for upcoming releases.

 

2. Configurations and Pre-requisites for Getting started:

You can download the add-in from the Microsoft Office Store. Please note that you need an SAP Analytics Cloud license to access your data with the add-in in Microsoft PowerPoint.

Deploy the add-in to Microsoft Office PowerPoint 365 web version.

2.1 Prerequisites:

SAP Analytics Cloud tenants you use with the add-in must have several trusted origins sites configured. To configure the trusted origins, sign-in to your tenant and choose System Administration -->App Integration --> Trusted Origins and add the following sites as trusted origins:

https://*.online.office.com 
https://*.online.office.de 
https://*.live.com 
https://*.sharepoint.com 
https://*.sap.com

2.2 Tenant Configuration:

SAP Analytics Cloud tenants can be hosted on different domains. You can use tenants with the standard domain hcs.cloud.sap  without any additional configuration.

Tenants of the other domains, for example, sapanalytics.cloud or hanacloudservices.cloud.sap, must be manually configured by following the below steps as an Microsoft365 administrator.

  1. Deploy the add-in as an administrator. For more information, please see 2.4 Deploy and publish the add-in as administrator section. In step 8 of the deployment, choose Just me and Deploy.
  2. Install the Microsoft PowerShell on your machine in case it is not yet installed. For more information, please see Microsoft Powershell at docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell.
  3. Install the Microsoft PowerShell module O365CentralizedAddInDeployment. For more information, please see O365CentralizedAddInDeployment at powershellgallery.com/packages/O365CentralizedAddInDeployment/
  4. Configure your SAP Analytics Cloud tenants. The following procedure shows the configuration for the two tenant examples https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud  and https://mycompany-test.eu10.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap 
  • Open the PowerShell and connect to your Office365 tenant by running the command Connect-OrganizationAddInService.
    A logon dialog appears where you log in with your Microsoft365 admin user.
  • Register all your tenants hosted on the domains sapanalytics.cloud and hanacloudservices.cloud.sap by running the command Set-OrganizationAddInOverrides:
  • Set-OrganizationAddInOverrides -ProductId 936cdfe6-bcc5-44f2-9213-0d9ab9dc905d -AppDomains "https://hcs.cloud.sap","https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud ","https://mycompany-test.eu10.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap "
  •  Add all your tenant URLs separated by a comma and enclosed by "" to the command. The tenants of type "https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud " and "https://mycompany-test.eu10.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap" must be adapted. The tenant "https://hcs.cloud.sap"doesn't need to be adapted, but it has to be part of the command.

Note that from time to time the PowerShell loses the connection to the Office365 admin center. Maybe you must reopen the PowerShell and reconnect to the admin center viaConnect-OrganizationAddInService.

After successful Set-OrganizationAddInOverrides command execution, you should get a result with the modified configuration.

    5. Deploy the add-in to your users.

After a successful tenant URL configuration, you can deploy the add-in to your users. Go to the Microsoft365 admin center and choose the add-in to add your users/groups (step 7 and 8 in the Section 2.4 Deploy and publish the add-in as administrator ).

 

2.3 Deploy the add-in as a user:

To Deploy the add-in as a user, please follow the below steps:

  1. Open the Microsoft 365 PowerPoint web version in a browser.
  2. Navigate to Home Tab--> Add- ins --> Select More Add-ins

   3. Choose "SAP Analytics Cloud" Add-in from the Admin Managed dialog or Store. 

   Click on Open in Powerpoint 

Now the Add-in is launched in Powerpoint, click on Accept and Continue

5. Now SAP Analytics Cloud add-in is available in your Microsoft PowerPoint Ribbon.

2.4 Deploy and publish the add-in as administrator: 

As an administrator, you can use Centralized Deployment in the Microsoft 365 admin center to publish the add-in to users and groups in your organization.

Please note that the Configurations for the add-in are a prerequisite for using the add-in. Refer 2.2 Tenant Configuration section. 

Before you start, please check if your organization and users meet the requirements to use centralized deployment. The admin and the users must have Exchange Online and active Exchange Online mailboxes, for example.

For more information, please see the Microsoft Office documentation at Determine if Centralized Deployment of add-ins works for your organization

 
  1. Open the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and sign in.

    You can open the admin center from Office 365. To do so, choose the App Launcher and Admin: 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
For more information about accessing and using the admin center, you can see the Microsoft Office documentation at About the Microsoft 365 admin center
 
1.Open the link admin.microsoft.com to access the admin center directly.
2.In the navigation menu, press Show all, then choose Settings Services and add-ins.

3.Choose Deploy Add-In at the top of the page.
4.Choose Next after reviewing the requirements.

5.Choose the option Choose from the Store.

6. Search for SAP Analytics Cloud for Powerpoint to find the add-in
7.Choose Add to publish the add-in to other users.
8.Specify the users to whom you want to deploy the add-in and choose Deploy.

The process may take a few minutes. Users might need to relaunch Office to see the add-in in the ribbon. No further client configuration is required.

For general information about deploying add-ins centralized, you can check the Microsoft Office documentation at Publish Office Add-ins using Centralized Deployment via the Office 365 admin center

 

3. SAP Analytics cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint Features:

3.1 Sign-in:

Once the Add-in is successfully deployed, SAP Analytics Cloud add-in will be added to the Ribbon of your Microsoft PowerPoint web version and will open with the sign-in page.

1. Click on Sign-in from the SAP Analytics Cloud Ribbon.
2. Enter the SAP Analytics Cloud URL Ex: https://mycompany-qm.us10.sapanalytics.cloud .

Note: “GO” button is enabled only if the URL is valid and meets all the technical requirements for the Add-in. you can also click on “Trouble signing in? “Option which will re directs to help page which provides more information about the requirements and configurations.

3. Click on “GO”
4. Sign-in Credentials are not required if single sign-on is enabled, or if you have accessed SAP Analytics Cloud through the same browser.
5. If Single Sign-on is not configured, A new window opens to sign in to your “SAP Analytics Cloud” Tenant.

 

6. Enter the SAP Analytics Cloud credentials and click on Logon.
7. After successful login, the add-in landing page is displayed with Explore and Favorite tabs.

3.2 Explore and Add a Widget:

Access your SAP Analytics Cloud stories and widgets through the Explore and Favorites sections.
 
   a.Recently Accessed Stories:
       •View recently accessed stories from SAP Analytics Cloud.
    b.Search for a Story:
       •Use the 'Search for a story' field to find specific stories.
    c.Favorite Stories:
      •You can Mark and Unmark the SAP Analytics Cloud Stories 
      •Browse all stories marked as Favorites in SAP Analytics Cloud under the Favorites tab.

Note:

If a story in the Recent Stories or Favorites or from a Search results page list is greyed out and you can’t select it. you will see an information icon explaining about the below reasons.

  1. SAP Analytics Cloud add-in supports only the Optimized stories.
  2. Optimized stories without scripting
  1. Choose a story from Recent Stories or Favorites or from a Search for a story” field. Ex: SAP_OAG_DSC_TSWSHIPMENT story is selected.
 
   2. All the Pages in the selected SAC story will be listed.
       Ex: Pages that are available in SAP_OAG_DSC_TSWSHIPMENT story are listed.
  3. Choose and expand any Page in the SAP Analytics Cloud story and all the available widgets in this story are listed.

   Ex: Expand “Supply Chain Performance Overview Page”. -à all the available widgets with the chart type icon list are displayed.

   4. Click on any available list of widgets. Ex: “Planned, Actual and Delta Transit time” widget.

Tip : you can re-size the add-in to see the complete chart information. 

 5. Click on Add widget to add this widget as image into the slide.

   

 Tip: you need to un-select any objects in the slide and then click on Add widget.

6. On Mouse over of the widget, you will notice an edit button which will enable you to interact and change the widget with different options “apply a filter, Drill up, Drill down …… “

7. SAP Analytics Cloud Add-in Saves the actual state of the widget as a Bookmark that you have modified and added into the slide.

8. On mouse over, you will be able to see the tooltip information of data and you can do a filter on data point selection.

 

 
9. You can add more widgets to the slide. For Ex: slide below now contains various types of SAP Analytics Cloud widgets.

 

3.3 Data Refresh in the Widgets:

You can refresh the widget's data whenever you need ensuring that your presentations always contain the most accurate and trusted data without the need to copy/paste the information from your SAP Analytics Cloud system again.

SAP Analytics Cloud Add-in does a data refresh of the actual state of the widget that you have modified and added into the slide.

Choose “RefreshData” tab from the ribbon and it will launch the Refresh Panel.  

Refresh page displays all slides only having the recently added Sap Analytics Cloud widgets. All the widgets are grouped by slide.

It displays the widget information with Name, Last Refresh time stamp and Title of the SAP analytics Cloud story widget.

  1. In the above example In Slide2, there are 7 SAP Analytics Cloud widgets are added as images.  You can choose a single widget or select All to refresh all widgets in this PowerPoint Presentation.
  2. Click on Refresh button to refresh the selected widgets.
  3. All the selected widgets will be refreshed with the latest data.

Note Only the data will be updated during a Data Refresh; any changes or modifications to the SAC widget will remain unaffected.

 

4. Sharing of PowerPoint Slide to different users:

Microsoft PowerPoint slides are usually shared to different users. Let’s how add-in works when the PowerPoint slides are shared.

  1. PowerPoint Shared with View Access:
    • When an admin creates a PowerPoint presentation, adds an SAC widget to a slide, and shares it with users who have view-only access, those users will only be able to see the SAC widgets as images since they do not have permission to install the Add-in.
  2. PowerPoint Shared with Edit Access:
    • When an admin creates a PowerPoint presentation, adds an SAC widget to a slide, and shares it with users who have edit access, those users can install the Add-in. Users with edit access and valid SAC credentials can refresh the SAC widgets in the presentation.

5. Supported Features and some restrictions in the initial release.

Supported Features :

  • SAP Analytics cloud add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint supports only Office 365 Web versions.
    • Microsoft 365 for Mac (Web)
    • Microsoft 365 for Windows (Web) 

Note: Desktop Version will be supported in upcoming releases. 

  1. Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge browsers
  2. SAC stories created using Acuired and Live Connection Data sources.

Restrictions : 

  1. Only supports Optimized SAP Analytics Cloud stories.
  2. Below list of features will not be available in add-in :
    • Data Analyzer
    • Digital Board room
    • Analytical application
    • Stories created in Classic mode. 
  3. The stories listed below are displayed in PowerPoint but cannot be used:
    • Stories that are created with Force Prompts - will be supported in Future
    • Stories with Scripting - will be supported in Future
    • Stories with Value Driver tree.
  4. Table widgets and Geo Maps - will be supported in Future

6. Tips and workarounds:

  1. Press the SAP logon button twice to access the Add-in Panel.
  2. Drag and resize the Panel to fully view the widget's data and legends.
  3. Keep widget images at their original size, as refreshing updates content but not size.
  4. Data Refresh option: Only data will be updated, not the formatting; and any another widget modifications won’t be retained.
  5. Make sure nothing is selected on the slide before clicking the Add Widget button.

7. Get SAP Support

If you have further questions, please open an incident in the SAP Support Portal on the component LOD-ANA-OF-PPA. 

References: 

  1. https://appsource.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/WA200007270?tab=Overview 
  2. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD_PPT 
  3. https://me.sap.com/notes/3496609 
  4. https://me.sap.com/notes/3289328 
  5. https://me.sap.com/notes/3496603 
  6. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD_PPT?locale=en-US

entry.title: OpenSAP sunset/end of life - move to SAP Learning what it means.
entry.content:
OpenSAP sunset/end of life - move to SAP Learning what it means.

6 minute read.

 

As many of you may already know OpenSAP, open.sap.com will no longer be the go to portal for SAP learning as all new offerings will be given on learning.sap.com instead. Tuesday 30th July 2024 has now been confirmed as the end of life for OpenSAP. With some courses on the platform finishing as recently as June 2024 the opportunity for self study and a certificate of participation may still be of interest to many as course materials can still be downloaded, including slides, transcripts and videos. Don’t have time?, don't worry, over 100 courses have been migrated to SAP Learning.

 

What is OpenSAP? In OpenSAP’s own words:

 

About openSAP

openSAP is SAP’s free learning platform for everyone interested in learning about SAP’s latest innovations and how to survive in the digital economy. openSAP Enterprise MOOCs are complete courses, and learners can earn a certificate to demonstrate the knowledge they’ve acquired. Complementary learning opportunities exist in the form of openSAP Podcasts and openSAP Microlearning.

Source: open.sap.com

 

OpenSAP, the sister platform to OpenHPI, open.hpi.de of the Hasso Plattner Institute (which shall continue to offer its unique brand of Information and Communication Technology courses in both English and German) has been around since 2013, has accompanied many of us through a large part of our professional journeys. Therefore it’s with some nostalgia that we say goodbye to the tried and tested format of video lectures, quizzes and end of week assignments usually comprising of 30 questions in a multiple choice format similar to those of SAP Certification.  The twist with OpenSAP was that the weekly assignments and final exams were negatively marked so a wrong answer actually subtracted points from your score rather than simply receiving no marks.

 

With regards to SAP Certification the move to SAP Learning also signals a unification and democratization of SAP’s learning content and Certification programme.  Due to SAP’s pledge to upskill 2 million tech professionals by 2025 no longer is SAP Certification only the consequence of taking costly SAP Academies booked through SAP Training, training.sap.com or SAP Learning Hub, learninghub.sap.com. Now thousands of learning journeys can be accessed free of charge at beginner, intermediate and advanced level with certificates of completion provided also free of charge.  Where an SAP Certification is relevant or desired the user can choose to purchase a subscription to SAP Certification Hub (guidance is provided directly from the courses that lead to certification) and program an examination attempt once they feel ready to do so.

 

The following is a list of the courses I have had bookmarked for some time and I’m now getting round to consuming their valuable content to both broaden and deepen my understanding of SAP’s Analytics solutions and the complementary technologies that are being offered amongst other front-line subjects.  Some of the content has been updated for the new platform where applicable notably the quizzes at the end of each section but the vast majority of the content has been migrated as is to SAP Learning site.  So you don’t have to rush to download everything from OpenSAP unless your particular course of interest has not been migrated in which case there may already be a new version of the course on SAP Learning. If a new course has been created on SAP Learning or an existing course migrated this is mentioned in the course details of the courses on OpenSAP with a link to the new material as pictured below.

My top picks:

 

Introduction to SAP Datasphere (Repeat), 2023

SAP Analytic Cloud sister product for Data warehousing (formerly SAP Data Warehouse Cloud).

https://learning.sap.com/courses/introduction-to-sap-datasphere?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Building AI and Sustainability Solutions on SAP BTP, 2023

Week 5 is Collaborative Enterprise Planning in SAP Analytic Cloud applied to the course case study, Bagnoli & Co..  Otherwise a good deep dive into SAP’s sustainability strategy and Business Technology Platform (BTP) capabilities in Artificial Intelligence using SAP AI Core.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/building-ai-and-sustainability-solutions-on-sap-btp?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Using the Optimizer for SQLScript Programming in SAP HANA, 2023

If you’ve worked with HANA connections in SAC this is a great resource on performance tuning.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/using-the-optimizer-for-sqlscript-programming-in-sap-hana?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Two-Tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 2018

One of the older offerings that caught my eye, unit 3 is on SAP Business Planning and Consolidation which is the precursor to SAC Planning so clients often ask that you are familiar with both solutions for content migration and hybrid environments.  The rest of the course is reinforcing your knowledge of SAP core processes on the cloud version of S/4 HANA.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/two-tier-erp-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Gain Experience with SAP S/4HANA – Logistics (Repeat), 2022

Data Analysts, Engineers and Scientists can all benefit from deeper understanding of the business processes that underpin the data they work with. Covers Order to Cash, Plan to Product, EWM, TM and Procure to Pay.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/gain-experience-with-sap-s-4hana-logistics?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Insights into Selected Logistics Innovations in SAP S/4HANA, 2022

As with the previous offering this course provides a deeper understanding of advances in business processes help us bring more know how to data analytics.  Covers SRM, MRP, EAM and EWM.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/insights-into-selected-logistics-innovations-in-sap-s-4hana?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Gain Experience with a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA (Repeat), 2022

Part three of OpenSAP’s courses on System conversion, this is still a hot topic despite SAP moving its end of maintenance support for SAP ECC back to December 31st 2027.  Particularly clients on enhancement pack 5 or earlier will see their support run out at the end of 2025.  All SAP professionals should be versed in what this entails.  All three courses are available on SAP Learning Site, also search for:

“Key Technical Topics in a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA” and 

“Key Functional Topics in a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA”

https://learning.sap.com/courses/gain-experience-with-a-system-conversion-to-sap-s-4hana?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course



Build Intelligent Data Apps with SAP HANA Cloud Multi-Model, 2023

Cloud is both the present and the future of many SAP services that are becoming must know knowledge for Application Developers, Consultants, Solution and Enterprise Architects.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/build-resilient-applications-on-sap-btp-with-amazon-web-services?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Build Resilient Applications on SAP BTP with Amazon Web Services, 2023

As the previous offering hyperscalers are both the present and the future of many SAP services therefore this is becoming a must know subject for Application Developers as well as Solution Architects, Consultants, Sales and Presales personnel.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/build-intelligent-data-apps-with-sap-hana-cloud-multi-model?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

What Can Blockchain Do for You?, 2022

A great breakdown of what Blockchain is and entails explained in largely none-technical terms to keep us all abreast of this new paradigm in the tech world.  This content has also been migrated to SAP Learning where any future updates to content can be added.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/what-can-blockchain-do-for-you?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Transform Your Business Processes with SAP Signavio and SAP Build, 2024

SAP Signavio is cutting edge technology for analyzing, automating and optimizing your SAP Business Processes.  Add SAP Build Apps and Graph to the mix and you have a low code option for creating front-end applications.  This particular course will also be available on the SAP Learning site if you prefer to pursue a certificate of course completion there along with a range of complementary courses on SAP Signavio.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/transform-your-business-processes-with-sap-signavio-and-sap-build?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Finally, we bid OpenSAP a nostalgic farewell but with renewed enthusiasm for continuing our learning journeys with a refreshed format, impetus and clearer path to SAP Certification.  As both a consumer of these courses and trainer myself I welcome any comments regarding your own experiences both with OpenSAP and SAP Learning, what you think they do well and what they might improve upon.  Happy learning!

 

#OpenSAP #SAPLearning #SAPCertification #SAPLearningTransformationStrategy

entry.title: Decoding the Future: Exclusive Insights from the FP&A Trends Survey 2024
entry.content:
SAP is thrilled to sponsor the FP&A Trends Survey 2024, garnering insights on financial planning and analysis from over 2,400 responses worldwide. As data's crucial role in decision-making accelerates, it's clear that data quality, technology adoption, and integration are keys to success.

According to the survey, 64% of organisations claim most decisions are now data-driven, increasing from last year's 52%. Yet, 35% of organisations still cite data quality and timeliness as significant obstacles to achieving data-driven decision-making. This underscores the need for continuous investment in improving data quality and integration processes.

The survey further revealed that 40% of organisations upgraded their systems in the past year, reflecting a trend towards modernising FP&A tools for improved data handling and result generation. However, Excel remains as the predominant planning application, underlining the challenge of transitioning towards more advanced platforms.

Underwhelmingly, the adoption of AI and Machine Learning within FP&A has dropped from 11% in 2021 to 6% in 2024, probably due to integration challenges and skills gaps. However, with 15% of organisations planning to adopt AI/ML within six months and 44% in the longer term, we still see the promise of AI/ML realisation in the future.

These insights are merely the tip of the iceberg from the comprehensive FP&A Trends Survey 2024. For a more detailed analysis and a deep dive into data-driven decision-making, forecasting, scenario planning, technology adoption and much more, kindly access the full survey here. 

The survey provides a wealth of information that is highly beneficial for any finance professional looking for current trends and best practices in FP&A. Don't miss out on this opportunity to learn and stay at the forefront of FP&A practices.

entry.title: 经典故事迁移到优化故事：表格的转换
entry.content:
简介

越来越多的客户在尝试将经典故事转换为优化故事，如果你的经典故事中有表格，那你有可能会遇到这个提示页面，告知您你的故事中有未使用优化演示的表格，因此不能转换。您需要进行一些操作后，才可以进行转换。

那么，你应该如何处理这个表格呢？此博客将介绍 SAP 分析云的表格功能的一些演变，以及如何成功地将含有表格的经典故事转换为优化故事。

 

什么是优化演示表

  1. 许多客户在日常工作中会使用 Microsoft Excel，更加熟悉数据的网格布局，也有很多用户喜欢、依赖于网络布局。因此，自 QRC 2021.3起，我们引入了“优化演示表”作为新的默认值，并扩展了显示网格的功能。
  2. 为故事构建方面提供更大的灵活性，以在 SAC 画布（和响应式）页面的网格中显示数据。这允许故事构建者使用画布（和响应式）页面的所有特性和功能。

与经典网格页面相比，使用 SAC 的优化演示表有许多优势。我们为优化演示表做了多项性能改进，包括更快的加载单元格内的统计图和阈值、平滑滚动、改进的链接分析使用等。如果您仍在使用网格页面设计故事，可以考虑将内容移动到优化演示表，这样终端用户可以享受到上述的性能改进。

此外，我们还可以使用画布（和响应式）页面的全部功能。表的用法允许用户在画布页面或响应式页面两者选其一使用。也就是说，用户可以使用故事模式下的所有可用微件。（网格演示中提供数据的）表微件旁边，可以在同一故事页面上添加统计图、图像、页面筛选器、数据操作等。

 

如何确认我的表格是否为优化演示表？

打开您的经典故事中的表格，打开生成器，在表结构一栏中找到优化演示选项。如果此项没有被勾选，说明你的表格未启用优化演示模式，这个表格无法转换成优化体验。

 

如何创建优化演示表，有什么好处？

  • 创建画布（或响应式）页面并创建一个表微件。确保要按照您的偏好或需求设置筛选器和钻取。我们将使用以下例子。前提条件：确保已选中“优化演示”复选框，此设置在表微件的生成器面板中。
  • 勾选显示“网格”和“列/行标题”

  • 通过拖拉可以调整表占据整个页面的大小。单击表微件右边框的加号/减号，我们可以轻松添加和调整列数。要在网格中添加行，可以在表微件的下边框中使用相同的加号/减号。

  • 现在利用网格，我们可以轻松地将表中的值复制粘贴到网格中，或者像此示例一样在公式栏中建模计算。

  • 最后，我们可以使用统计图、页面筛选器、数据操作等填充画布页面。可以观察到，表的功能类似于在画布页面中嵌入一个网格页面。因此，我们可以利用画布模式的成熟功能。以下截图中，我们添加了几个微件，以说明表（含关联网格）如何集成到画布页面中。

 

功能亮点

  • 表的全屏模式：全屏模式下，表会自动展开。当终端用户正在查看一个适合全屏的表时，此功能特别有用，当然它也同时提供数据的网格表示。

  • 自适应列宽：在每个表生成器面板的“表结构”中，可以勾选或取消“自适应列宽度”选项。如果我们取消勾选，用户可以很容易地调整列宽和行高。这与终端用户使用 Microsoft Excel 的习惯非常相似。

 

为什么优化体验故事中没有这个选项？

在优化体验故事中，所有的表格都自动开启了优化演示模式，你无需手动开启，即可享受网格等高级功能。这也是为什么我们要求您的经典故事中的表必须要开启优化演示模式后，才可以被转换成优化体验故事。

 

总结

以上，本博客详细介绍了 SAC 中优化演示表的演变过程、功能和优势。我们也深入了解了将含有表格的经典故事转化为优化体验故事过程中可能遇到的问题，以及相应的解决方法。如果您正在使用优化体验故事，优化演示表的功能已经自动可用。

 

原作者：@johnannes_huhn

原文链接：Best Practice: Leverage grid presentation of SAP A... - SAP Community

entry.title: コーポレートサステナビリティの推進：クラウド ERP Financials に沿った温室効果ガス排出量計画
entry.content:
このブログは、Driving Corporate Sustainability: Greenhouse Gas Emission Planning along Cloud ERP Financialsを日本語訳したものになります。

 

はじめに

SAP Analytics Cloud　の2024年第1四半期ビジネスコンテンツリリースでは、クラウド ERP の温室効果ガス排出量も含めるために、「統合財務計画」のビジネスコンテンツを拡張しています。この拡張は、企業が将来の企業および製品のカーボンフットプリントを計画するのに役立ち、SAP のグリーン元帳に向けた重要なステップの１つです。

※ビジネスコンテンツ「統合財務計画」の既存の計画モデルに新たに温室効果ガスカテゴリ等のディメンションが追加されています。また、計画モデルの拡張に合わせて既存のデータアクションのロジックも変更されています。

この投稿では、これが企業事業計画プロセスにどのように組み込まれているかを紹介します。さらに、販売、生産、および財務データを使用してシナリオをシミュレートし、さまざまなドライバパラメータ (原材料やエネルギー源の排出係数など) に基づいて予想される結果を予測する方法についても詳しく説明します。企業は、このデータを使用してサステナビリティ目標を達成し、特定のアクション領域を特定し、財務上の影響を管理することができます。

サステナビリティメトリクスを財務計画プロセスに統合することで、サステナビリティ目標の達成、コストの最適化、および会社の長期的な価値の創出に役立ちます。

図 1：グリーン元帳に沿った GHG 計画

この図は、SAP Analytics Cloud による温室効果ガス排出量計画で販売数量、品目資源、または費用計画を使用して製品および企業フットプリントを計算する方法を示しています。ソリューションを SAP Sustainability Footprint Management と統合して、計画プロセスに必要な製品排出量データを取得することができます。S/4HANA の財務プロセスで実際の温室効果ガス排出量を計上する予定の SAP Green Ledger(SAP Carbon Accounting Hub) と組み合わせることで、SAP Analytics Cloud はグリーン元帳に向けた重要な一歩となります。

主な利点:

  1. ビジネス運営および計画モデルの一環としての温室効果ガス排出量の運用化
  2. 財務に関連する企業および製品のフットプリントを計画し、適宜計画および最適化
  3. 販売計画や生産計画などのエンタープライズ事業計画に統合された温室効果ガス排出量計画を設定し、温室効果ガス排出量に関連する財務リスクとコストを特定
  4. 回復力、持続可能性、収益性の高いビジネスモデルを実現し、企業の長期的な価値を保護
  5. さまざまなシナリオに基づいて、企業の温室効果ガス排出量をシミュレートおよび予測
  6. 温室効果ガス排出量を、財務計画および予測と緊密に連携

 

温室効果ガス排出量計画の開始

SAP Analytics Cloud の統合財務計画を開始するために、SAP ではデモデータが付属するビジネスコンテンツを提供しています。このデモデータを使用すると、SAP Analytics Cloud で財務計画ストーリーと機能を即座に探索することができます。さらに、ビジネスコンテンツには、SAP Analytics Cloud へのデータのインポート、および SAP S/4HANA Cloud または SAP S/4HANA へのデータのエクスポートを可能にする事前定義済の接続が含まれています。ビジネスコンテンツを理解したら、デモデータを削除し、SAP S/4HANA および S/4HANA Cloud システムの独自のマスタデータおよび実績データに置き換えることができます。

本ブログで紹介するSAP Analytics Cloud のビジネスコンテンツの名称は、 Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA (xP&A – Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud)です。

ビジネスコンテンツは、SAP Analytics Cloud コンテンツライブラリから利用できます。コンテンツネットワークからビジネスコンテンツを取得する方法に関するビデオを視聴し、SAC ビジネスコンテンツをインストールおよび更新する方法、およびSAP Analytics Cloudのビジネスコンテンツの探し方とインストール手順を確認してください。

 

概要 - ランディングページおよびデータフロー

温室効果ガス排出量計画のプロセスは、財務計画に完全に統合されています。そのため、統合財務計画のランディングページが温室効果ガス排出量計画の領域によって拡張されました。排出係数と評価を計画するためのストーリーと、配分、活動率、および間接費に関するストーリーが提供されます。その結果、完全な 温室効果ガス排出のスコープに関するステートメントと、財務構造に沿った CO2 換算量を含む財務諸表およびキャッシュフロー計算書が生成されます。

図 2：IFP および GHG ランディングページ

以下の図は、温室効果ガス排出量計画ストーリーのデータフローを示しています。これには、ランディングページからもアクセスできます。

図 3：GHG データフロー

計画プロセスを容易にするために、コンテンツ拡張には 2 つの追加ストーリーが付属しています。1 つ目は、温室効果ガス排出係数の計画および評価ストーリーです。これにより、消費金額および活動タイプごとに、構成品目数量単位および原価センタ係数ごとに原材料の係数を入力することができます。これらの係数は、原材料および原価センタごとの二酸化炭素換算量 (CO2e) の計算に使用されます。2 つ目は、原価センタおよび製品への温室効果ガス排出量配分ストーリーです。これにより、原材料および原価センタの CO2e を計算して、二酸化炭素換算量 (CO2e) 活動および製品のレートを決定することができます。これらのレートは、進行中の期間中、つまり実際の CO2e 値が判明する前に、製品、原価センタ、および財務諸表を評価するために使用できます。

温室効果ガス排出量計画のインタラクティブなプロセスグラフィックを参照してください。

図 4：GHG 計画プロセスの対話式グラフィック

 

温室効果ガス排出係数の計画および評価

温室効果ガス排出係数計画および評価のストーリーでは、原材料および原価センタ業務の係数を入力して、その二酸化炭素換算量 (CO2e) を計算することができます。ストーリーはさまざまなタブに分かれており、対話式プロセスグラフィックのプロセスステップを反映しています。

図 5：GHG 排出係数の計画と評価

関連する計画から CO2e をコピーし、原価センタ CO2e を計算し、原材料 CO2e を計算するためのデータアクションが提供されます。計算機能には、費用ベース係数と CO2e の対応する金額を乗算し、活動基準係数を数量で乗算することが含まれます。原材料の場合、CO2e は G/L 勘定ごとに数量に CO2e を乗算して計算されます。

図 6: 原価センタ CO2e の計算

結果は、二酸化炭素換算量の合計を反映した温室効果ガススコープステートメントです。

図 7：GHG スコープステートメント

 

原価センタおよび製品への温室効果ガス排出配分

原価センタおよび製品への温室効果ガス排出量配分ストーリーでは、製品および活動率を見積もるために、原材料および原価センタの CO2 換算 (CO2e) が計算されます。これらは、実績 CO2e 値が判明する前の特定期間における製品、原価センタ、および財務諸表の評価に使用されます。必須の前提条件には、温室効果ガス (GHG) 排出係数の計画および評価が含まれます。

図 8：コストセンタおよび製品への温室効果ガス排出量の配分

このツールには、原価センタ CO2e、活動依存原価センタ CO2e、合計活動消費量、CO2e 活動率、製品 CO2e 率、製造間接費、製品への販売および管理 CO2e 配分、原材料 CO2e、製品 CO2e、および合計 CO2e など、さまざまなデータを計算および表示するためのタブが含まれています。最後に、CO2e 活動率を SAP S/4HANA Cloud の ACCOSTRATE にエクスポート(詳細は製品ヘルプ参照)することができます。

図 9：CO2e 活動率の計算

図 10：製品 CO2e の計算

'データアクション' を使用して、原価センタ CO2e の原価センタへの配分、活動単位ごとの原価センタ CO2e の計算、合計製造間接費 CO2e のコピーによる原価センタの適切な貸方転記、CO2e 支出係数と販売数量の乗算による合計 CO2e 間接費の計算などのさまざまな計算が実行されます。

図 11：原価センタ CO2e の表示

最後に、原価センタ販売および管理 CO2e は、計画総収益に基づいてプラントおよび製品に割り当てられ、完全な計画結果が 3 つの報告書に反映されます。これらは、GHG スコープステートメント、財務諸表、および CO2e を含むキャッシュフロー計算書です。

図 12：スコープ、財務およびキャッシュフロー計算書としての合計 CO2e の表示

 

まとめ

温室効果ガス排出量計画を SAP S/4HANA 統合財務計画に組み込むことで、企業は経済的サステナビリティ目標とともにサステナビリティ戦略を実行できるようになります。SAP Analytics Cloud の機能に基づくこの高度な計画、シミュレーション、および予測ツールにより、温室効果ガス管理が拡張され、温室効果ガス排出量に関連する財務リスクとコストに関するインサイトが提供されます。全体として、持続可能なビジネスプラクティスと SAP の グリーン元帳 に向けた重要なステップです。

entry.title: Get Total number of rows in a SAC Model
entry.content:
Below Steps will guide the user to get the total row count for a SAC Model. In case of a Planning Model it will give the total row count of the model by summing up all the versions.

 

  • Open Developer Tools(F12) and Click on Network

 

  • Select the Model for which you want the total row count and wait for the screen to load.
  • Type 'model' in the filterbox as below.

 

  • Select the 'model?tenant=X' action and click Preview.

Rows will be displayed under the Preview under rows

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud – Allocations Management Migration Update
entry.content:
With QRC1 2024, we restricted the usage of the legacy Allocation Designer for designing new allocation processes and offered a manual migration path to data actions (more details can be found here). Continuing our product journey of gradually discontinuing the legacy Allocation Designer and running structured allocation processes as an integral part of data actions, we reached the next milestone of automatically migrating all legacy allocations to data actions.  

QRC3 - Important Updates Coming

With the SAP Analytics Cloud upgrade to QRC3, the legacy allocation processes and steps will be automatically copied and migrated to data actions, facilitating an easy transition from the legacy allocation functionality. Using the data actions going forward for defining and executing the structured allocation processes brings additional benefits, such as parameterizable data action triggers, multi-action execution, and automated planning processes (visit SAP help for more details).

The migration will ensure a smooth transition by maintaining the following:

  • The data action representations of the legacy allocations will retain all existing relations between allocation steps, processes and data actions embedded legacy allocations.
  • Each allocation process is copied and migrated to a dedicated data action. Its properties, like name and description, are transferred to this data action for easy retrieval. The process's legacy allocation steps are migrated as data action steps.
  • The legacy allocation steps used multiple times in different allocation processes and/or data actions will be created as nested data actions.
  • The privileges transfer is part of the migration. This means the corresponding created data actions will be governed by the same user- and team- privileges as the original legacy allocations. A Planning Professional license is required to access, edit, and execute data actions. 

The automatically created data actions will be accessible from the newly created Files > My Files > Public > Structured Allocations folder.

Image 1 Migrated legacy allocations

 Note: You will retain view-only access to legacy allocations through the Allocation Legacy Designer. However, editing legacy allocation processes or steps will no longer be possible.

Image 2 Using the Legacy Designer does not allow creating new allocations

Although the execution of legacy allocations is still supported, we strongly advise updating your legacy structured allocation execution workflows. As a reminder, users triggering legacy allocations from stories will receive a notification advising them to transition to data actions.

Image 3 Message displayed upon legacy allocation execution

The final phase of discontinuing legacy allocations is expected with the QRC4 update. Then, we will no longer support the execution of legacy allocations. Please use the automatically migrated data action representations or manually migrate your allocations in the oncoming release period (QRC3 until QRC4) and update your planning workflows accordingly. We plan to retire the legacy designer completely with QRC1 in 2025.

Please use the Commenting section if you have any questions or require assistance with the structure allocations transition to data actions!

entry.title: Execute Data Action on Publish Data Event
entry.content:
As an SAC Planning Developer, you might need to run some custom code or Data Actions before or right after the user clicks the Publish Data button. I would like to highlight two possible solutions for this use case:

  1. If your Story is relatively small and simple, and you are willing to sacrifice the official "Publish Data" button, you can remove it by going to the View Time Settings and removing it from the Toolbar. Once you have done this, you can add a custom publish button with your coding to each of the Story Pages. It's not ideal, but it works.

  2. The second semi-automated option would be to execute your coding after the user hits the official Publish Data button or publishes a Private Version to the Public one.
    Handling Revert Data button: A challenge is that if the user clicks the 'Publish Data' -> 'Revert Data' button, it will also trigger the script. This is because both actions - publishing and reverting - result in a clean version after having unpublished data. Therefore, it is not possible to distinguish between Publish and Revert in this context.

Now I would like to focus on the solution for the second point. It needs to be implemented in each of the tabs used for planning separately. The upside is that the user does not have to click anything. To achieve this, you can check if there are any private versions in the onInitialization event of the page and save them for future use.

 

 

 

// Save private versions
var privateVersions = TabCostCalc.getPlanning().getPrivateVersions();

// Get private version IDs
for (var l = 0; l < privateVersions.length; l++) {
   	strArrPrivateVersionCc.push(privateVersions[l].getId());
}

 

 

 

Now you need to check on every table refresh if the private version existed before and disappeared. You can implement this in the onResultChanged event. Additionally, you need to add a check if the official version was in a 'dirty' state and started being clean. This would indicate that someone either has reverted the changes or published them.

 

 

 

var activeVersionMembers = icVersion.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
var userPrivateVersions = YourTableId.getPlanning().getPrivateVersions();
var executingCustomLogicMessage = "Hang tight! We're executing custom logic.";

if (activeVersionMembers.length > 0) {
    var publicPlanVersion = YourTableId.getPlanning().getPublicVersion(activeVersionMembers[0].description);
}

if (privateVersionArray.length > 0) {
    var privateVersionIds = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);

    // Collect private version IDs
    for (var i = 0; i < userPrivateVersions.length; i++) {
        privateVersionIds.push(userPrivateVersions[i].getId());
    }

    // Verify if any private versions have been removed
    for (var j = 0; j < privateVersionArray.length; j++) {
        var missingVersions = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);
        var existingVersions = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);

        if (!privateVersionArray.includes(privateVersionIds[j])) {
            missingVersions.push(privateVersionIds[j]);
            Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, "Version " + privateVersionArray[j] + " was published or removed.");
            Application.showBusyIndicator(executingCustomLogicMessage);

            // Add your code here to execute after a private version has been published to public

        } else {
            existingVersions.push(privateVersionArray[j]);
        }
    }

    // Refresh private version IDs
    while (privateVersionArray.length > 0) {
        privateVersionArray.pop();
    }
    for (var k = 0; k < userPrivateVersions.length; k++) {
        privateVersionArray.push(userPrivateVersions[k].getId());
    }
}

if (publicPlanVersion) {
    if (publicPlanVersion.isDirty()) {
        isVersionDirty = true;
        // Notify the user that they may need to publish their data
        PublishText.setVisible(true);
    } else if (isVersionDirty) {
        if (!publicPlanVersion.isDirty()) {
            Application.showBusyIndicator(executingCustomLogicMessage);

            // Add your code here to execute after the results have been published and the version is no longer "dirty"

            // Reset the indicator for private versions after publishing results
            isVersionDirty = false;
            PublishText.setVisible(false);
        }
    }
}

 

 

 

An example scenario in which this option could prove useful is in case you would like to materialize your data for analytics after each publication of the planning values.

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2024.15版功能更新
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能（BI）、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍了 SAP 分析云 2024.15版本的新功能。这些新功能已经在 SAP 分析云 FastTrack 客户的系统上线。对于 SAP 分析云季度发布周期（QRC）客户，此版本及其功能将作为 QRC 2024年第3季度版本的一部分提供。在此处可以查看 QRC 发布时间表。 

 

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本1.0.409，以利用所有数据采集类型。

代理的最低版本仍为 1.0.75，但以下情况除外：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库的版本 1.0.99
  • SAP universe （UNX） 的版本 1.0.365（SAP 分析云代理需要其他设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明 3262098）
  • SAP Business Warehouse （BW） 的版本 1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View 的版本 1.0.235
  • File Server版本 1.0.248

有关详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

有关SAP BW与SAP分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅SAP Note 2541557了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP分析云的 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 实时访问Universe时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的简易部署工具包。

  • 注意：为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本的工具包可供下载。

有关云代理版本变更管理详情，请参阅 SAP Note 3264839。

 

更新亮点

  • 面向计划和分析的人工智能
    • Just Ask 附加用户权限
  • 数据集成
    • 弃用 Salesforce 连接
  • 企业计划
    • 故事 API：对组合任务的审核者添加拒绝支持
  • 故事可拓展性
    • 故事设计者查看视图中的增强性能工具

 

面向计划和分析的人工智能

JUST ASK更多用户权限

为了向 Just Ask 管理员提供更精细的访问控制，我们实施了以下权限：

  • 执行：允许用户使用Just Ask。工具的灯泡图标将显示在页面右上角。
  • 维护：具有此权限的用户除了可以访问工具外，还可以上传未明确索引为 Just Ask的模型。

 

数据集成

不再支持 Salesforce 连接

SAP 分析云不再支持与 Salesforce 的数据连接。要从 Salesforce 系统导入数据，请使用通过 JDBC 或 APOS 实时数据网关的 SQL 数据库连接。请参阅 SAP 说明 3402695。

 

企业计划

故事 API：对组合任务的审核者添加拒绝支持

在组合任务中，"decline() "和 "canUserDecline() "这两个函数原本就适用于组合任务的分派者，现在也适用于组合任务的审核者。 这样，API就能与用户界面行为保持一致。 分配者和审核者都可以在用户界面上拒绝任务，现在还可以通过故事或分析应用（通过脚本编写提供这些功能）拒绝任务。

 

故事可拓展性

故事设计者查看视图中的增强性能工具

在优化故事体验中，用户可以在查看视图中使用性能分析工具，该工具会根据故事中的资产提供加载顺序、故事统计数据和建议，供你分析故事表现。

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP 知识库文章。

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP 分析云即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: Time-Based Filtering in SAC Stories: Using Model Variables for Measure Restriction
entry.content:
Consider a scenario where planning data needs to be compared to actuals, and the actuals include adjustments (i.e., Actuals = Actuals + Adjustments). Depending on the input control selection, it may be necessary to exclude certain periods from these adjustments. This can be achieved in a few simple steps:

Create a Model Variable
First, create a model variable with the appropriate data type to handle the time-based offset.

Use the Variable in a Restricted Measure
Use this variable as an offset in the restricted measure for adjustments:

 

IF([d/Date].[p/FISCAL_CALPERIOD]>=['ActualsOffsetStartPeriod'],[AdjustmentsMeasure] ,NULL )​​

 

Initialize the Variable on the Screen Load
Set an initial value for the model variable when the screen loads, preferably in the landing page so the table does not refresh multiple times:

 

Application.getFileDataSource("ModelId").setVariableValue("ActualsOffsetPeriod", "Sample Fiscal Period");​​

 

Update the Variable Based on Input Control Selection
Determine which planning scenario was selected in the input control, execute custom logic to obtain the correct Fiscal Period for the offset, and update the model variable accordingly:

 

var strSelectedScenario = icSampleInputControlSelection.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers()[0].id;
var strChosenFiscPeriod = yourLibrary.getSampleFiscPeriod(strSelectedScenario);

Application.getFileDataSource("ModelId").setVariableValue("ActualsOffsetStartPeriod", strChosenFiscPeriod );​

 

By following these steps, it is possible to dynamically restrict a measure in an SAC Model using a variable value passed from a custom script.

entry.title: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
The Need for Upload Functionality

Planning is often done in a heterogeneous landscape – using different tools, products, landscapes etc.  Thus, an exchange of data between systems is essential and not all these data loads can or should be done by the administrator. Many of our customers have been voicing the need for a simple way for the end users to be able to easily upload plan data from csv or Excel files.

Established Work Arounds

SAC has been offering a file upload with the file import in the data management area of the model. But what the customers were asking for was a simpler solution for the end user that can be triggered directly from the story.

Thus, we will introduce a new end user friendly upload of fact data from a csv or Excel files with QRC3 2024.

As the need for an end user driven upload functionality has been quite large there are several blogs in this community describing solutions for a file upload based on custom widgets (and calling the Data Import Service to post the data to the SAC system), in particular https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-upload-data-to-a-public-or-private-planning-version-using-a-file/ba-p/13576559

With the new standard solution customers we can now replace those custom solutions (which are not maintained by SAP) with a proper solution in the product. We strongly recommend our customers to migrate to the standard solution as SAP is not going to provide any further enhancements/corrections to the custom widget mentioned in the blog above (which has the state of ‘sample coding’ anyway) from now on.

Details of the New Solution

Let us have a look at how the new file upload works. We have three main personas involved in the upload:

  • Modeler: uses a template file to create the re-useable upload job, defines job parameters and mappings from file columns to system fields, defines wrangling (if necessary),
  • Story designer: creates the file upload starter (previously called trigger) in the story using the predefined upload job, defines version behavior and publish behavior,
  • End User: chooses upload file (Excel or csv) and version (if applicable).

Let us have a closer look at the functionality now.

Defining the upload job

We have introduced a new entry in the data management area of the model – the upload job. This is the job type that is used for the new file upload. Please note that the new file upload is only available for new models but not for "classical" account models.

To create a new upload job, the modeler must upload a sample file. This file does not need to (but can) contain real data. The sample file must have the same structure as the upload file the end user will use later.

The upload job uses the same wrangling screen as the import jobs. Various transformations can be defined, for the uploaded data, and it is also possible to un-pivot the file. Thus, it is possible to upload excel/csv files that use a drill of one dimension (typically the time) in the columns. Please note that the column headers must be the same when defining and when executing the file upload and that the number of columns must be identical.

In the mapping screen the columns of the file are mapped to the system dimensions. Instead of mapping each target dimension selected dimensions can also be set to fixed (single) values. If a measure is not mapped during this stage, then the existing measure values in the system will not be changed. Thus, it is possible to upload only selected measures.

The version needs to be mapped to a single value and cannot be filled from the file. The reason for this is that currently the system will always upload to a single private or public edit version. The system does not upload directly into public versions – a publish step is always necessary. This publish step can either be done by the end user or can be triggered automatically.

The version set in the mapping dialog is only a DEFAULT version. This default can be overruled by the story designer or/and by the end user (depending on the settings in the trigger).

In addition, the modeler can decide in the general settings whether the upload should append or overwrite the data or whether a sign flip should be performed.

Please keep in mind that the upload functionality at this stage is for transaction data only. At a later stage we also want to introduce an upload for master data.

Using the upload job in the story

The story designer can now use the upload job in a data upload starter. The publish behavior (no publish or automatic publish) can be defined in the data upload starter just as in the data action starter.

The story designer can also define the version handling – whether the end user should be able to change the version or not, and whether the default defined in the job, a specific member (option member selector), or a member picked from story filter or an input control should be used.

In addition, the story designer can use two scripts that are executed before and after the file upload.

 

Performing the upload

Now the end user can use the upload in the story. When pressing the button, the user is prompted for the file name and – depending on the settings in the starter – the version.

The user will be informed by a toast message about the success of the upload. If certain records have been rejected the user will be informed and there is the possibility to download those rejected records. In addition, the user can check the success of the file upload in the notifications.

There are various reasons why a record can be rejected during an upload. Among technical reasons (like wrong data format) the system is checking against the master data, data locks, authorizations, and validations defined in the system.

Please note that during an upload all correct records will be posted to the selected version. Currently there is no ‘all or nothing’ behavior that rejects an upload if there is at least one erroneous record. If such a behavior is desired the end user can revert the data upload in the history once there are rejected records.

In addition to the user also the modeler can check the performed uploads. In the data management tab there is the data timeline that shows the latest executions of all job types. The summary of the failed records can also be downloaded from there.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans. This blog post introduces the new features and enhancements of the SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024 Quarterly Release (QRC).

Join our SAP Product Experts in the video below as they demonstrate some of the features highlighted in Q3 2024.

 

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.409 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
Version 1.0.91 for SAP universe (UNX)
Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Important: With every quarterly release update, there are required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud via SNOTES. This will ensure that the SAP BW back and font ends are in sync. To learn which SNOTES are required for implementation, refer to the XML-File attached in SAP Note 2541557.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Administrative-level control of sharing public and private files | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Allowing administrators to disable welcome e-mails for new users | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Plan Auditing – purging audit data based on other filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Tenant Links Tab Update
    • Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP
  • AI for Planning and Analytics
    • Just ask - natural language query | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
      • Just Ask: Display Dimension Attributes
      • Just Ask: Prompt Guide
      • Just Ask Comparison
      • Just Ask: Additional User Rights
  • Business Content Delivery
    • Business content packages for cash liquidity, portfolio financial planning, and financial planning | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Center Availability
    • Data center availability in Israel for Google Cloud Platform | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data center availability in Japan for Google Cloud Platform | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Exploration
    • Data Analyzer - Shortcut Link | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Data Analyzer - Share an insight from within the tool | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data analyzer – new functionality for the table context menu | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Live SAP BW - Report-Report Interface (RRI) | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Unit positions for SAP HANA based models
    • Support SAP Datasphere folders in Select Model dialog
    • Calculations Dialog: Conversion Functions
    • Calculations Dialog: LOG, LOG10, CEIL, ROUND, TRUNC
    • Calculation Editor Enhancements
    • Context Menu Enhancements in Builder Panel / Available Objects list
    • Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer
  • Data Integration
    • Live SAP BW - Report-Report Interface (RRI) | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Blended charts for SAP Business Warehouse | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Currency rates - import from additional sources | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Export API - Delta Difference | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Plan Entry – data file upload (end-user) | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Deprecate Salesforce Connection
    • Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections
    • Enable credential sharing for IBP and Generic ODATA connections
  • Data Modeling
    • Standard Unit Conversion | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
  • Embedded Analytics
    • Integration with Microsoft PowerPoint | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    •  
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Advanced Formulas – supporting the “any” hierarchy parameter | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Data Actions - Mapping Strategy "Property" | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Data actions and multi actions - support for account or measure input control parameters | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Data Actions – migration of existing allocation design time | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Data Export API - Delta Difference | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Currency rates - import from additional sources | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Multi Actions – creation and update by BI users | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Plan Auditing – purging audit data based on other filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Plan Entry – data file upload (end-user) | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Planning Process Management – administrator option to group calendar notifications | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Standard Unit Conversion | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
    • Value Driver Tree - styling flexibility for cascading style sheets | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Table: support of advanced filter with unbooked mode | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Story API: Add decline support for reviewer on composite task
  • Enterprise Reporting
    • Multi Actions – creation and update by BI users | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Table: support of advanced filter with unbooked mode | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Add-in for Microsoft Excel
      • Support offline mode for cell-based formulas
      • Null Suppression
      • Frontend Calculations referencing members
      • Continuing member selector updates in Microsoft Excel – Select a Range | (Road Map Explorer)
      • Defining and applying conditional formatting in Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Integration of add-in Excel files for SAP Analytics Cloud with the file repository | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Providing live support for SAP Hana Cloud with Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
      • Integration with Microsoft PowerPoint | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video) (Blog)
  • Mobile Analytics
    • Biometric support in Android mobile | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
  • Story Design
    • Optimized design experience story – blended charts with SAP BW, time series forecasting, smart grouping, and more | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
      • Blended charts for SAP Business Warehouse | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Smart Grouping in Charts in the optimized story experience
      • Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Apply Scaled Measures across All Included Pages in PDF/PPTX Exporting
      • Section export printing | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Forecast in chart support in optimized story experience
    • Descriptions for story calculations | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • New support in lite viewer | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Retrieving non-input-ready variables as dynamic text or through script API | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Table: support of advanced filter with unbooked mode | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Vertical filter panel – reordering of page filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Improved Linked Analysis with Visualization Drill
    • Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations
    • Apply default color palette to Table in Cell Charts
    • Public URL API support for BW Hierarchies
  • Story Extensibility
    • Composite Enhancement: Expose Widget Property as Composite Property Sheet | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Cross-page pop-up widget | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Custom widget: showing unbooked data values | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Custom widget: support for calculated and account-based dimensions | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Increasing the number of the hosted custom widgets and widget add-ons to 50 | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Enhance Performance Tool for Story Designer View time Scenarios
    • Retrieving non-input-ready variables as dynamic text or through script API | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Save story with scripts | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Widget Customization Add-on to support legend | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Presentation
    • Table: support of advanced filter with unbooked mode | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
  • User Collaboration
    • Show last comment in comment widget | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Story comment configuration | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Schedule optimized story experience in PowerPoint format | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Schedule and deliver story content within the email body | (SAP Road Map Explorer) 

 

Business Content Delivery

 

Business content packages for cash liquidity, portfolio financial planning, and financial planning  

Reduce the time spent implementing SAP Analytics Cloud by adopting new, prebuilt data models and stories.

Updated packages for industries and lines of business for Q3 2024 include: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

New industry and line-of-business content packages:

  • Cash liquidity planning
  • Consensus net revenue planning
  • Portfolio financial planning for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Portfolio financial planning for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Rapid financial planning and analysis for SAP S/4HANA (SAP Best Practices)
  • Rapid sales planning and analysis for SAP S/4HANA (SAP Best Practices)
  • Rapid workforce planning and analysis for SAP S/4HANA (SAP Best Practices)
  • Receivables management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Best Practices)
  • Control analytics for SAP governance, risk, and compliance solutions (SAP GRC solutions)
  • Cross-model functionality for integrated financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
  • Group financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
  • Integrated financial planning for SAP S/4HANA

Lear more about SAP Analytics Business Content here.

 

Data Center Availability

 

Data center availability in Israel for Google Cloud Platform

Customer now have more choices in data center locations and hyperscaler platforms through support for deployment on Google Cloud Platform in Israel. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data center availability in Japan for Google Cloud Platform  

Customer now have more choices in data center locations and hyperscaler platforms through support for deployment on Google Cloud Platform in Japan. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data Integration

 

Currency rates - import from additional sources

Users can now directly import currency rates from SAP S/4HANA, SAP HANA, or OData data sources. This improves the flexibility and simplifies the workflow for planning and analytics scenarios where currency conversion is required. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Add-in for Microsoft Excel

 

Continuing member selector updates in Microsoft Excel – Select a Range

The member-selector in the add-in for Microsoft Excel now supports selecting a range of members to define in the filter. This is done using the RANGE operator. (Road Map Explorer)

This feature will be available only when the members are visible in “flat list mode” in the member selector.

Please note this is a preview and will be released in in Q3 2024.

 

Defining and applying conditional formatting in Microsoft Excel

We are introducing a new tab for styling for a certain table than enables the following: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • Show thresholds for a certain table
  • Create, edit, and delete thresholds, and change them to active or inactive

Please note this is a preview and will be released in in Q3 2024.

 

Integration of add-in Excel files for SAP Analytics Cloud with the file repository

Users will be able to save add-in for Microsoft Excel files directly in SAP Analytics Cloud’s file repository. From there, you can organize the files in folder and set permissions for them, as with other file repository objects.

Please note this is a preview and will be released in in Q3 2024. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Providing live support for SAP Hana Cloud with Microsoft Excel

SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft office will support live connection to SAP HANA Cloud. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Please note this is a preview and will be released in in Q3 2024.

 

Story Design

 

Blended charts for SAP Business Warehouse

Blended charts are now supported in the optimized story experience for SAP BW data models, closing feature parity gaps in the classic experience. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

The following features are supported:

  • Activate/Deactivate BW Conditions on both Primary and linked models
  • ID Format
  • Description Format
  • Expand/Collapse
  • Set Hierarchy Dimension Level

There was an improvement made with SAP BW conditions where we now differentiate between the Conditions with a hover over tooltip. 

We do not support the list of features that were unsupported in the Classic Experience:

  • Show Totals
  • Two structure
  • Calculated Dimension
  • Measure Based Filter
  • Date/Time Dimension as link dimension
  • Filter across multiple models
  • Rank disabled when hierarchy dimension used in chart binding

 

Story Extensibility

 

Increasing the number of the hosted custom widgets and widget add-ons to 50  

Developers can now host up to 50 custom widgets and 50 widget add-ons. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. For more details on these features, please visit the SAP Analytics Cloud Help Portal. You can also visit the SAP Road Map Explorer to see the upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: New Feature - Ability to Disable Welcome Emails
entry.content:
Overview

We will be introducing a new feature that gives an Administrator the ability to disable Welcome emails for newly created users. Welcome emails are sent as a very last step during the user creation process to provide information and links to SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Datasphere.

SAC Welcome Email: 

Datasphere Welcome Email:

 

What Business Problem are we trying to solve?

Some reasons why Administrators and organizations want this feature:

  • Fine-grained control over email communications for users
  • Reducing email traffic to users as a company policy
  • During testing, you may want to load real users into the SAC/DSP system but you don’t want them receiving email alerts. 
  • Mass imports of users through SCIM

 

What is the solution?

In QRC3 2024, you will see a new option to disable welcome emails in the System > Administration > Notifications area (in both SAC and Datasphere)

 

The default value is ON for welcome emails. If you toggle this off, then emails will not be sent when a user is created through the UI, CSV Upload, API (SCIM V1/V2) and SAML Dynamic User Creation. 

 

FAQ’s

Q: How does a new user activate / set a password for their account without a Welcome email?

A: In this scenario, SAC is configured with the SAP provided IDP such as https://cloudanalytics.accounts.ondemand.com/ and click Forgot Password. This will send a separate activation email.

 

Feedback

Provide feedback and comments directly in this blog. I would be happy to hear from you

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2024 年第三季度更新介绍
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能（BI）、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍 SAP 分析云 2024 年第三季度的季度发布 (QRC) 的新功能和增强功能。

在链接的视频中，SAP 产品专家将向您展示 2024 年第三季度的部分重点功能。

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本1.0.409，以利用所有数据采集类型。

代理的最低版本仍为 1.0.75，但以下情况除外：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库的版本 1.0.99
  • SAP Universe （UNX） 的版本 1.0.91（SAP 分析云代理需要其他设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明 3262098）
  • SAP Business Warehouse （BW） 的版本 1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View 的版本 1.0.235
  • File Server版本 1.0.248

有关详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

注意：每季度发布更新时，都需要通过 SNOTES 更新 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云的集成。这将确保 SAP BW 的后端和前端同步。要了解实施时需要哪些 SNOTES，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 中所附的 XML 文件。

关于SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 以了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 与 SAP 分析云来实时访问 Universe 时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921 以了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的“简易部署工具箱”。

注意：为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对 Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本可供下载。 

关于云代理的版本变更管理细节，请参考 SAP Note 3264839。

 

更新亮点

  • 系统和服务管理
    • 对共享公共文件和私有文件的管理级别控制 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 禁用对新用户的欢迎邮件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
    • 数据审计：能够根据其他过滤器删除审计数据 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 租户链接选项卡更新
    • 防止丢失通过 BTP 意外删除的 SAP 分析云租户
  • 面向计划和分析的人工智能
    • Just Ask-自然语言查询 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • Just Ask：显示维度属性
      • Just Ask：提示指南
      • Just Ask 比较
      • Just Ask：附加用户权限
  • 业务内容交付
    • 面向现金流动性、产品组合财务计划和财务计划的业务内容包 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据中心可用性
    • 为以色列提供 Google Cloud Platform 的数据中心 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 为日本提供 Google Cloud Platform 的数据中心 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据发掘
    • 数据分析器 - 快捷链接 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 数据分析器- 在工具内分享见解
    • 数据分析器- 表上下文菜单的新功能 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 实时 SAP BW - 报表至报表接口 (RRI) |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
    • 基于 SAP HANA 模型的单位位置
    • 在“选择模型”对话框中支持 SAP Datasphere 文件夹
    • 计算对话框：转换函数
    • 计算对话框：LOG、LOG10、CEIL、ROUND、TRUNC
    • 计算编辑器增强功能
    • 生成器面板/可用对象列表中的上下文菜单增强功能
    • 在数据分析器中为两个结构BW查询添加对结构成员的计算
  • 数据集成
    • 实时 SAP BW - 报表至报表接口 (RRI) |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
    • SAP Business Warehouse 的混合统计图 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 货币汇率 - 从其他来源导入 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据导出 API – 增量差异 | （SAP 路线图浏览器） (博客)
    • 计划输入 - 数据文件上传（最终用户） |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
    • 弃用 Salesforce 连接
    • 支持基于 OData 连接的 X509 证书身份验证
    • 为 IBP 和通用 ODATA 连接启用凭据共享
  • 数据建模
    • 标准单位转换 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
  • 嵌入式分析
    • 与 Microsoft PowerPoint 集成 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)  (博客)
  • 企业计划
    • 高级公式 - 支持层次结构参数设置为“任何”   |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 映射策略“属性” |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 数据操作和多步操作 - 支持账户或度量输入控件的参数 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 数据操作 - 迁移现有的分配设计器 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 数据导出 API – 增量差异 | （SAP 路线图浏览器） (博客)
    • 货币汇率 - 从其他来源导入 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 多步操作 – 由 BI 用户创建和更新 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 数据审计：能够根据其他过滤器删除审计数据 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 计划输入 - 数据文件上传（最终用户） |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
    • 计划流程管理 - 对日历通知进行分组的管理员选项 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 标准单位转换 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) (博客)
    • 价值动因树 - 灵活设置级联样式表的样式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 支持在“留言”中使用单位转换
    • 表：支持无值模式的高级筛选器 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 故事 API：对组合任务的审核者添加拒绝支持
  • 企业报表
    • 多步操作 – 由 BI 用户创建和更新 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 表：支持无值模式的高级筛选器 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
  • Microsoft Office 集成
    • Microsoft Excel 插件
      • 支持基于单元格的公式的离线模式 | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • 消空
      • 引用成员的前端计算
      • 在 Microsoft Excel 中继续更新成员选择器– 选择范围 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 在 Microsoft Excel 中定义并应用条件格式设置 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • SAP 分析云的加载项 Excel 文件与文件资源库的集成 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • Microsoft Excel 加载项与 SAP Hana Cloud 实时连接 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • Microsoft PowerPoint 加载项
    • 与 Microsoft PowerPoint 集成 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)  (博客)
  • 移动分析
    • Android 移动设备中支持生物识别 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
  • 故事设计
    • 优化设计体验故事 - 支持 SAP BW 的混合统计图、时间序列预测、智能分组等功能 （SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • SAP Business Warehouse 的混合统计图  | （SAP 路线图浏览器)
      • 优化故事体验中的图表智能分组
      • 优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片
      • 在 PDF/PPTX 导出中对所有包含的页面应用缩放度量
      • 区段导出打印 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 优化故事体验中支持统计图的时序预测
    • 故事计算的描述 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 精简查看器中的新支持 |（
    • 以动态文本或脚本 API 的形式检索非输入就绪变量 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 表：支持无值模式的高级筛选器 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 垂直筛选器面板 - 页面筛选器重新排序 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 改进包含链接分析的可视化钻取
    • 在地理可视化对象中，精简版查看器现在支持按维着色的气泡图层
    • 将默认调色板应用于表中的单元格统计图
    • BW 层次结构的公共 URL API 支持
  • 故事可拓展性
    • 组合增强：将微件属性暴露为组合属性表 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 跨页面弹出窗口微件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 自定义微件增强：展示无值数据 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 在自定义微件中创建计算维度或基于帐户的维度 |（SAP 路线图浏览器) 
    • 将可存储的自定义微件和微件加载项的数量增加到 50 个 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 故事设计者查看视图中的增强性能工具
    • 以动态文本或脚本 API 的形式检索非输入就绪变量 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 保存带有脚本的故事 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 支持图例的自定义微件插件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
  • 故事演示
    • 表：支持无值模式的高级筛选器 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
  • 用户协作
    • 在留言微件中显示最近添加的留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 故事留言配置 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 以 PowerPoint 格式计划发布优化故事体验 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 通过电子邮件正文形式计划发布故事内容

 

 

业务内容交付

针对现金流动性、产品组合财务计划和财务计划的业务内容包

采用新的预构建数据模型和故事，减少实施 SAP 分析云所花费的时间。

2024 年第 3 季度针对行业和业务线更新的软件包包括：（SAP 路线图浏览器）

新的行业和业务线内容包：

  • 现金流动性计划
  • 共同点净收入计划
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公有云版本）的产品组合财务计划
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（私有云版本）的产品组合财务计划
  • SAP S/4HANA 的快速财务计划和分析（SAP最佳实践）
  • SAP S/4HANA 的快速销售计划和分析（SAP最佳实践）
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的快速劳动力计划和分析（SAP最佳实践）
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud 的应收账款管理（SAP最佳实践）
  • 面向 SAP 治理、风险与合规解决方案（SAP GRC 解决方案）的控制分析
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的集成财务计划的跨模型功能
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的集团财务计划
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的集成财务计划

点击此处，了解有关 SAP Analytics 业务内容的更多信息。

数据中心可用性

为以色列提供 Google Cloud Platform 的数据中心可用性

现在，通过支持在以色列的 Google Cloud Platform 上部署，客户可以在数据中心位置和 hyperscaler 平台做出更多选择。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

为日本提供 Google Cloud Platform 的数据中心可用性

通过支持在日本的 Google Cloud Platform 上部署，客户现在可以在数据中心位置和 hyperscaler 平台做出更多选择。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

数据集成

货币汇率-从其他源导入

用户现在可以直接从 SAP S/4HANA、SAP HANA 或 OData 数据源导入汇率。这提高了灵活性，并简化了需要货币转换的计划和分析场景的工作流。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

Microsoft Office 集成

Microsoft Excel 加载项

在 Microsoft Excel 中继续更新成员选择器 – 选择范围

Microsoft Excel 加载项中的成员选择器现在支持选择成员范围。此操作通过使用 RANGE 运算符完成。（SAP路线图浏览器）

仅当成员在成员选择器的“不分层列表模式”中可见时，此功能才可用。

请注意这只是一个预览，此功能将在 2024 年第 3 季度发布。

在 Microsoft Excel 中定义并应用条件格式设置

我们引入了针对每张表的新的样式设置标签，支持以下功能：（SAP 路线图浏览器）

  • 显示特定表的阈值
  • 创建、编辑和删除阈值，并将其更改为活动或非活动状态

请注意这只是一个预览，此功能将在 2024 年第 3 季度发布。

 

SAP 分析云的加载项 Excel 文件与文件资源库的集成

用户将能够直接在 SAP 分析云的文件资源库中保存 Microsoft Excel 文件的加载项。在此，您可以在文件夹中组织文件并为其设置权限，与其他文件资源库对象一样。

请注意这只是一个预览，此功能将在 2024 年第 3 季度发布。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

Microsoft Excel 加载项与 SAP Hana Cloud 实时连接

SAP 分析云的 Microsoft Office 加载项将支持与 SAP HANA Cloud 进行实时连接。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

请注意这只是一个预览，此功能将在 2024 年第 3 季度发布。

 

故事设计

SAP Business Warehouse 的混合统计图

现在，使用 SAP BW 数据模型的优化故事体验支持混合统计图，缩小了与经典体验中的功能差距。（SAP Road Map Explorer）

此更新支持以下功能：

  • 在主模型和链接模型上激活/取消激活 BW 条件
  • ID 的格式
  • 描述的格式
  • 展开/折叠
  • 设置层次结构维度级别

我们对 SAP BW 的状态进行了改进，现在我们通过将鼠标悬停在工具提示上的状态进行了区分。

我们不支持经典体验中不支持的功能列表：

  • 显示总计
  • 两个结构
  • 计算所得维
  • 基于度量的过滤器
  • 日期/时间维作为链接维
  • 跨多个模型筛选
  • 在统计图绑定中使用层次结构维时禁用排名

故事可扩展性

将可存储的自定义微件和微件加载项的数量增加到 50 个

开发者现在最多可以存储 50 个自定义微件和 50 个微件加载项。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP 知识库文章。

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的中文 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP 分析云即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 8th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast URL: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/08/sap-developer-news-august-8th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Kick-off LATAM SAP CodeJam roadshow and SAP Stammtisch

  • SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2024 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷 🇨🇱 🇦🇷 🇨🇴 (August 8th - 21st 2024): https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2024-latin-america-edition-august-8th-21st-2024/ba-p/13710015
  • SAP Stammtisch: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-stammtisch/eb-p/stammtisch

ABAP Open Source Namespaces

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-abap-open-source-namespaces/ba-p/13779488

July 2024 | CAP

  • SAP Cloud Application Programming Model release for July 2024: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/jul24

What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024

  • Harmonized release calendar for SAP Cloud products: https://me.sap.com/notes/2888562
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Release & Delivery Schedule: https://me.sap.com/notes/2728183
  • What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-analytics-cloud-q3-2024/ba-p/13777526
  • SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024 Release's Features with Experts & Demos: https://youtu.be/zgtutlXkd9c?si=e6Ej01woxhig7z8f

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Kick-off LATAM SAP CodeJam roadshow and SAP Stammtisch

1:16 ABAP Open Source Namespaces

2:05 CAP July Release

3:32 What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024

TRANSCRIPT

[Josh and Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. We made it as promised to Latin America. The first of our 12 events over the next two weeks. That's right. We have two CodeJams and one Stammtisch per city. And you can't wait to start today with the Event Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite CodeJam. And then a great Stammtisch this evening, followed by an SAP Build Apps CodeJam on Friday, before we head out to Santiago, Chile and our other stops on the road show. Now, when it comes to these coding events, we want to make sure that we are all learning. That's you, our audience, learning about Asset and Technologies. And that's us, the Developer Advocates. We are learning from you, the audience, our community, what the CodeJam means to you as an attendee. So be ready to answer these kind of questions from us at the CodeJam so that we can ensure these are the best events possible. And of course, be ready to go. Ciao from Sao Paulo.

[Rich] Hey, folks. Rich Heilman here. Last week, Sebastian Wolf introduced ABAP Open Source Namespaces. And traditionally, ABAP open source artifacts are published with names belonging in the customer namespace, so beginning with Y or Z, but of course this leads to naming collisions and all other challenges. Now with ABAP open source namespaces, developers can apply for their very own custom namespace to use for their open source ABAP projects. Sebastian has all of the details in his blog post with links to just about everything you would need to get started with your very own namespace. Taking a look at the list so far, and I see a few out there already, so make sure to grab your namespace today.

[Ajay] Hello, developers. We saw a major release for Cloud Application Programming Model in June. As of July, 2024, a CAP release is now available. In CDS language updates, the is-active entity in the context of Draft-enabled entities can now be used in expression annotations.
